items.q: What is the main function of dietary fibre?
items.correct: Aiding digestion and promoting bowel health.
items.why: Students think all carbohydrates give energy. Fibre is a carb humans can't digest, so it provides NO direct energy. It bulks stools, slows glucose absorption, and feeds gut bacteria.

items.correct: Proteins denaturing and bonding to form a solid structure when heated.
items.misconception: The egg shell breaking when boiled.
items.why: Students think coagulation is something physical happening to the shell. It's PROTEIN CHEMISTRY: heat unfolds the proteins, which then bond together, turning liquid egg into solid - what makes a fried egg set.

items.q: Are local foods always more sustainable than imported ones?
items.correct: Not always - production methods can outweigh transport emissions.
items.misconception: Yes - shorter distance always means lower emissions.
items.random_wrong: No - imported food is always better.
items.why: Students apply 'shorter is better' too simply. A heated UK greenhouse tomato may have higher emissions than a Spanish field tomato trucked north. Lifecycle analysis matters more than miles alone.
The answer examiners want is: Wholegrain bread. “Sweets” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many calories in 1g of fat?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 9. “20” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many calories per gram of fat?
items.why: The real figure is 9. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
